events.event: Victor Chemical Company began operating the phosphorus production facility
Facility operated by Victor Chemical Company, later Stauffer Chemical Company. Produced elemental phosphorus from phosphate ore.
Stauffer Chemical Company ceased operations at the facility
Stauffer Chemical Company decommissioned and dismantled the facilities
EPA listed the site on the National Priorities List (NPL). Exact listing date not yet confirmed from Federal Register.
Stauffer Chemical Company formed Stauffer Management Company, which owns the site and is the potentially responsible party (PRP)
PRP removed on-site phosphorus water and disposed of it at an off-site facility
EPA issued the Record of Decision (ROD) for Operable Unit 1 — the cleanup plan
First Explanation of Significant Differences (ESD) issued, modifying the cleanup plan
EPA modified the cleanup plan through four ESDs. Remedy components: excavation, consolidation, capping, groundwater cutoff wall, institutional controls

PRP conducted major cleanup: excavated/consolidated 220,000+ cubic yards of contaminated material; installed two caps (42 acres); installed groundwater cutoff wall (2,600+ linear feet); restored Meyers Cove; constructed sea wall (1,300+ linear feet); installed fencing
First Five-Year Review published. Protectiveness determination not yet extracted.
Second Five-Year Review published. Remedy determined protective of human health and the environment.
Third Five-Year Review published. Protectiveness determination not yet extracted.

operational_history.production_type: Elemental phosphorus from phosphate ore mined in Florida

contaminant_profile.groundwater_contaminants: Some of the above contaminants also found in groundwater
contaminant_profile.notes: Detailed contaminant concentrations and plume extent not yet extracted — require ROD document review

components: Excavation of contaminated material exceeding residential cleanup standards
Consolidation of contaminated material in main pond area, slag area, and/or other on-site areas
Construction of cap meeting Florida Administrative Code Section 62-701.600.5(g) over consolidation area
Construction of groundwater cutoff wall to reduce contaminant migration from former wastewater ponds
Institutional controls: deed restrictions, land use ordinances, physical barriers, surficial aquifer water supply well permitting prohibitions

controls: Deed restrictions prohibiting groundwater use at the site
Deed restrictions prohibiting future residential uses
Future industrial or commercial uses possible
institutional_controls.source: EPA Cleanup Activities — Current Site Status section

current_status.protectiveness: Remedy is protective of human health and the environment
ongoing_activities: Maintenance of grounds and capped areas
current_status.construction_complete: Inferred from 2011 soil cleanup; formal SEMS Construction Complete status: not yet confirmed from NPL list query

monitoring_continuity.notes: Cleanup Activities page states current activities include groundwater monitoring. Detailed monitoring data requires FYR document review.

phases.pre_npl_gap: 1983-1994 (11 years between dismantling and NPL listing)

pending_extractions: Exact NPL listing date (month/day/year) from Federal Register or ROD document
ROD detailed remedy selection and OU structure
ESD modification details for each of 4 ESDs
Groundwater monitoring: start date, well network, frequency, parameters, trends
SEMS Construction Complete status from NPL list
SEMS Human Exposure Under Control status from NPL list
SEMS Groundwater Migration Under Control status from NPL list
Health & Environment page for exposure pathway data
Operable Units page for OU structure
